idea.title: Word Snake
idea.hook: Eat letters to grow your snake — spell real words for power boosts, avoid your own tail.
idea.prototype: Classic top-down snake on a 20x15 grid. Letters scatter on empty cells as pellets. Snake moves continuously at 7 cells/sec, controlled by 4 arrow keys (desktop) or 4-direction swipe (mobile). Eating a letter appends to a 'word buffer' shown top-right. When the buffer contains a real word (3+ letters in EN/RU dictionary), grant +50 score, +5 length, and 1.5s invincibility. Self-collision = GAMEOVER. Edge-wrap to keep the loop forgiving early. Endless mode with persistent best.
idea.why_novel: Tim has 0 word/typing/spelling games across 70 shipped titles — fills an entire genre gap. Mutation of classic snake where the growth-as-pressure mechanic is reframed: the bonus for being clever (forming a real word) is MORE body to dodge, not less. Inversion: word-game reward becomes survival cost.
idea.non_timer_pressure: Resource scarcity (shrinking free cells) + physical pressure (own growing tail). No countdown. The pressure compounds monotonically — every pickup adds a segment, every word adds 5 more. Mirrors classic snake's free-cells-to-zero loop.
idea.lose_condition: Snake head cell index equals any body segment cell index (self-collision), checked every grid step. Invincibility frames briefly disable the check after a word bonus.

sources.title: Tim's portfolio has 0 word/typing/spelling games (70 shipped)
sources.snippet: This game fills a typing/word-game gap in Tim's portfolio (0 word games out of 70 shipped). It's intentionally NOT typing-based — letter-eating works on mobile.

sources.title: Balance and narrative remain reusable production skills
sources.snippet: Add a one-page balance sheet to each game: object sizes, spawn rates, score values, fail/reward timings. — Reused here as the tunables block in DESIGN.md.

sources.title: Yandex Словесные (Word) category is healthy but English-style word-eat-grow is rare
sources.snippet: Most Yandex word games are crossword / find-the-word / hangman variants — a snake + vocabulary cross-pollination is not a common entry in the category.

sources.title: Grid-based mechanics are a strong fit for Yandex moderation
sources.snippet: Grid-aligned movement avoids physics edge cases that have caused Yandex rejections; same-grid letter pellets are visually clean for both screenshots + screenshot moderation pass.

synthesizer_brief: Leader brief: fill a typing/word-game gap in Tim's 70-game portfolio with a non-typing letter-eating mechanic that works on mobile. Stay vanilla JS canvas, lean on classic snake bones, inline a small EN+RU word list so the loop works both regionally.

alternatives.title: Type Sprint
alternatives.hook: Type the falling word before it hits the ground; chain combos for speed boosts.
alternatives.why_novel: Pure typing — but works poorly on mobile, which is why Word Snake (letter-eating) was preferred.

alternatives.title: Letter Tetris
alternatives.hook: Stack falling letters and clear rows by spelling words across them.
alternatives.why_novel: Word-formation on a falling grid — but conflicts visually with shipped 25_wobble_stack and 33_brutalist_stack stacking metaphors.

alternatives.title: Anagram Roulette
alternatives.hook: A wheel of 7 letters spins; tap to lock; spell the longest valid word before time runs out.
alternatives.why_novel: Higher-skill word game — but timer-based, which violates the non-timer-pressure rule.

alternatives.title: Word Bridge
alternatives.hook: Lay tiles spelling words to bridge a gap before your hero falls.
alternatives.why_novel: Word-building under platformer pressure — but design complexity is too high for v0.1 mechanic prototype.
